programs.name: Aquatic Fitness Conference and Instructor Certification
programs.description: The City of Toronto and WaterArt International are cohosting a unique opportunity for anyone interested in aquatic fitness. The conference offers a great opportunity to be introduced to new techniques and learn about emerging trends in the aquatic fitness industry. Enjoy a variety of workshop sessions in both shallow and deep water, as well as theory workshops.The Shallow/Deep Aquatic Fitness Instructor Certification provides the opportunity to complete the theoretical portion of becoming a certified Aquatic Fitness Instructor. Skills will be taught through a combination of classroom and in-pool sessions. Practical and written examinations will be components of this certification.For more information on this activity, visit: https://www.toronto.ca/explore-enjoy/parks-recreation/program-activities/leadership-employment-readiness/aquatic-fitness-certifications/For employment with the City of Toronto, Standard First Aid CPR-C is required, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Cooking
programs.description: In this camp, you will learn the art of cooking. On the menu are basic skills like measuring, cutting, and mixing as well as kitchen safety, nutrition and healthy eating.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your cooking skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.Efforts will be made to accommodate dietary restrictions but we cannot make any guarantees.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Soccer
programs.description: Calling all soccer fans! Through practice and play, you will build on skills like kicking, passing and heading while learning the rules of the game an practicing respectful play. In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your soccer skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics, or workshops to further enhance the camp program.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness Conference and Instructor Certification
programs.description: The City of Toronto and WaterArt International are cohosting a unique opportunity for anyone interested in aquatic fitness. The conference offers a great opportunity to be introduced to new techniques and learn about emerging trends in the aquatic fitness industry. Enjoy a variety of workshop sessions in both shallow and deep water, as well as theory workshops.The Shallow/Deep Aquatic Fitness Instructor Certification provides the opportunity to complete the theoretical portion of becoming a certified Aquatic Fitness Instructor. Skills will be taught through a combination of classroom and in-pool sessions. Practical and written examinations will be components of this certification.For more information on this activity, visit: https://www.toronto.ca/explore-enjoy/parks-recreation/program-activities/leadership-employment-readiness/aquatic-fitness-certifications/For employment with the City of Toronto, Standard First Aid CPR-C is required, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.location: Joseph J. Piccininni Cmty Rec Centre

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Gymnastics
programs.description: Flexibility, strength, coordination and balance, gymnastics has it all. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness Conference and Instructor Certification
programs.description: The City of Toronto and WaterArt International are cohosting a unique opportunity for anyone interested in aquatic fitness. The conference offers a great opportunity to be introduced to new techniques and learn about emerging trends in the aquatic fitness industry. Enjoy a variety of workshop sessions in both shallow and deep water, as well as theory workshops.The Shallow/Deep Aquatic Fitness Instructor Certification provides the opportunity to complete the theoretical portion of becoming a certified Aquatic Fitness Instructor. Skills will be taught through a combination of classroom and in-pool sessions. Practical and written examinations will be components of this certification.For more information on this activity, visit: https://www.toronto.ca/explore-enjoy/parks-recreation/program-activities/leadership-employment-readiness/aquatic-fitness-certifications/For employment with the City of Toronto, Standard First Aid CPR-C is required, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness Conference and Instructor Certification
programs.description: The City of Toronto and WaterArt International are cohosting a unique opportunity for anyone interested in aquatic fitness. The conference offers a great opportunity to be introduced to new techniques and learn about emerging trends in the aquatic fitness industry. Enjoy a variety of workshop sessions in both shallow and deep water, as well as theory workshops.The Shallow/Deep Aquatic Fitness Instructor Certification provides the opportunity to complete the theoretical portion of becoming a certified Aquatic Fitness Instructor. Skills will be taught through a combination of classroom and in-pool sessions. Practical and written examinations will be components of this certification.For more information on this activity, visit: https://www.toronto.ca/explore-enjoy/parks-recreation/program-activities/leadership-employment-readiness/aquatic-fitness-certifications/For employment with the City of Toronto, Standard First Aid CPR-C is required, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Bronze Cross Recert
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. This course is for candidates looking to recertify their expired or expiring Bronze Cross certification. Participants will have the opportunity to review and update their techniques to maintain their current certification. Candidates will be required to perform the skills taught in Bronze Cross and must bring proof of original certification.Prerequisite: Bronze Cross and Emergency First Aid with CPR-B (current or expired) or Standard First Aid with CPR-C  from an approved provider.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Sports
programs.description: Calling all sports fans! In this camp you will have the opportunity to learn the fundamentals of play in a variety of sports. Through drills, practice and games, enhance your skills and have some fun.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your sport skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: Emergency First Aid (CPR B)

programs.name: Examiner Course
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. The Examiner Course is the first step in a three-step process to become certified as an Examiner for the Lifesaving Society. The Examiner Course prepares candidates to apprentice successfully as an Examiner on the exam of their choice, and it builds on the evaluation experience that candidates have attained in instructor evaluated awards and content.Prerequisite: Current instructor certification for the stream they are seeking examiner certification and being an experienced instructor, having taught or co-taught at least one course in that stream.Equipment: Bring the Award Guide(s) for the streams you wish to become an examiner in.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Games: Chess

programs.name: National Lifeguard - Pool
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. National Lifeguard education is designed to develop a sound understanding of lifeguarding principles, good judgment and a mature and responsible attitude toward the role of the lifeguard. The course develops the basic lifeguarding skills, principles and decision-making processes that will assist the lifeguard to evaluate and adapt to different aquatic facilities and emergencies.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 15 years old, and Bronze Cross, and Standard First Aid with CPR-C certification (current or expired) from an approved provider.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Games: Chess

programs.name: National Lifeguard - Pool
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. National Lifeguard education is designed to develop a sound understanding of lifeguarding principles, good judgment and a mature and responsible attitude toward the role of the lifeguard. The course develops the basic lifeguarding skills, principles and decision-making processes that will assist the lifeguard to evaluate and adapt to different aquatic facilities and emergencies.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 15 years old, and Bronze Cross, and Standard First Aid with CPR-C certification (current or expired) from an approved provider.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: National Lifeguard - Pool Recert
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. programs.name: Pool Operator - Level 1

programs.name: Swim and Lifesaving Instructor
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. Participants learn to organize, plan, teach and evaluate swimming, lifesaving and first aid skills. Discover and develop skills in the areas of class management, teaching, evaluation, leadership, communication and administration. With the successful completion of this course, candidates will be qualified to instruct the Toronto Learn to Swim program, the Lifesaving Society Swim for Life program, Emergency First Aid with CPR-B, Rookie, Ranger and Star Patrol as well as Bronze Star, Medallion and Cross.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 15 years old and Bronze Cross (current or expired).For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered with the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Yoga: Chair
programs.description: Gain all the benefits of yoga with the added support of a chair or your walker. Combining gentle stretches and traditional poses, you will improve your flexibility, strength, breathing and relaxation with every movement.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness Instructor: Specialty - Water Walking
programs.description: The Aquatic Fitness Instructor Water Walking Certification course is offered by WaterART Fitness International. The water walking program reviews ways to improve gait and provides a variety of stretching, strengthening and cardiovascular conditioning exercises. Learning a variety of walking patterns and progressions will provide additional core, posture, balance, and mobility benefits.Skills will be taught through a combination of classroom and in-pool sessions. This certification provides instructors with the skills they require to teach an engaging and safe aquatic fitness class in shallow water. Practical and written examinations will be components of this certification.Prerequisite: Current Aquatic Fitness Instructor Certification (Shallow/Deep)For more information on this activity, visit: https://www.toronto.ca/explore-enjoy/parks-recreation/program-activities/leadership-employment-readiness/aquatic-fitness-certifications/For employment with the City of Toronto, Standard First Aid CPR-C is required, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Bronze Medallion & Emergency First Aid (CPR B)
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. Bronze Medallion challenges the candidate both mentally and physically. Judgment, knowledge, skill and fitness – the four components of water rescue – form the basis of Bronze Medallion training. Candidates acquire the assessment and problem-solving skills needed to make good decisions in, on, and around the water. Bronze Medallion is a prerequisite for assistant lifeguard training in Bronze Cross. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 12 minutes or less by the course exam date. The Emergency First Aid (CPR-B) certification teaches victim assessment, CPR and addressing choking, as well as first aid treatment of external bleeding, heart attack, stroke and wound and burns. This course generally offered in an intensive weekend format.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 13 years old or Bronze Star. Candidates with Bronze Star, that are under 13 years of age, can register for this course by calling 416-396-7378 or visiting an in-person registration site.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Bronze Medallion, Cross & Standard First Aid (CPR C)
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. This is an intensive program designed for strong swimmers. The Bronze Medallion certification teaches how to manage complex water rescues through the development of physical fitness and judgment skills. The Bronze Cross certification teaches the difference between lifesaving and lifeguarding, the principles of emergency procedures and teamwork. The Standard First Aid (CPR C) certification provides comprehensive first aid training that covers spinal injuries, heat or cold injuries, bone and joint injuries, abdominal and chest injuries, burns, medical emergencies, and automated external defibrillator usage. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 13 years old or Bronze Star. Candidates with Bronze Star, that are under 13 years of age, can register for this course by calling 416-396-7378 or visiting an in-person registration site.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. Learn comprehensive first aid training covering spinal injuries, heat/cold injuries, bone and joint injuries, abdominal and chest injuries, burns, medical emergencies, and the proper use of automated external defibrillators.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered with the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Standard First Aid (CPR C) Recert
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. This recertification course provides a review of first aid and CPR skills. Practical skills are assessed through scenarios and knowledge will be tested through a written evaluation.Prerequisite: Current Lifesaving Society Standard First Aid with CPR-C (only current certifications from the Lifesaving Society will be accepted).For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered with the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Strength and Conditioning
programs.description: Get your muscles moving in the Strength and Conditioning class. With an emphasis on full body conditioning, exercises will target key muscle groups including arms, legs, abdominals and chest to improve you overall endurance and flexibility.

programs.name: Swim and Lifesaving Instructor
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. Participants learn to organize, plan, teach and evaluate swimming, lifesaving and first aid skills. Discover and develop skills in the areas of class management, teaching, evaluation, leadership, communication and administration. With the successful completion of this course, candidates will be qualified to instruct the Toronto Learn to Swim program, the Lifesaving Society Swim for Life program, Emergency First Aid with CPR-B, Rookie, Ranger and Star Patrol as well as Bronze Star, Medallion and Cross.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 15 years old and Bronze Cross (current or expired).For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered with the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Workshop: Breakfast with the Birds
programs.description: This is a workshop based on the neighborhood birds you don’t want to miss! . You first start the morning with a pancake breakfast and then play themed games and make crafts, this drop-in activity is the perfect choice. Activities take place outside so please be dressed for the weather.

programs.name: Yoga
programs.description: Relax your body and mind with yoga.  Learn and practice yoga poses and postures to improve your balance, breathing, flexibility and strength.

Please note that campers must be registered at the same location for extended hours as the activity they have registered in. Refer to the camp program charts for times, fees and locations.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Dance Basics
programs.description: Explore and enjoy a variety of dance styles including ballet, jazz, hip hop and contemporary. As you practice basic steps and proper technique, you will build on flexibility, strength and endurance.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Environmental Adventure
programs.description: Get ready to explore the great outdoors. You will learn about and connect with the environment through hiking, crafts and nature games.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on the  environment, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Gymnastics
programs.description: Flexibility, strength, coordination and balance, gymnastics has it all. In this camp, you will learn and practice basic skills such as front roll progressions, handstands, and cartwheels, as well as floor skills like leaps jumps and turns.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your gymnastics skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics or workshops to further enhance the camp.

programs.name: CampTO Plus: Soccer
programs.description: Calling all soccer fans! Through practice and play, you will build on skills like kicking, passing and heading while learning the rules of the game an practicing respectful play.In addition to the minimum two hours a day focusing on developing and fine-tuning your soccer skills, you will also enjoy games, arts, sports and swim days dependent on weather and camp location. A trip or on-site special experience will be included, such as themed events, clinics, or workshops to further enhance the camp program.
